mappings.rationaleAI: The Spanish Civil War (1936-39) covers the Popular Front, German and Italian intervention, and its role as a prelude to WWII, directly matching A.3.b on the Popular Front and the Spanish Civil War.

The Second Sino-Japanese War (1937-1945) covers Japanese aggression in China, the Nanjing Massacre, Communist guerrilla warfare, and Japan's eventual surrender, directly matching A.3.a on Japanese seizure and B.2 on the war in Asia and the Pacific.

The Manhattan Project covers the development of nuclear weapons, the bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, and the legacy for the Cold War arms race, directly matching B.6 on burgeoning military technology and C.2.a.i on atomic and hydrogen bombs.

mappings.episodeTitle: The Economic Consequences of the Peace
mappings.rationaleAI: Keynes's Economic Consequences of the Peace critiques the Treaty of Versailles reparations and their political consequences, directly matching A.1.b on diplomacy after the Paris Peace Conference and crises concerning the peace settlement.
Hindenburg's appointment of Hitler as chancellor in 1933 directly relates to A.4.c on the establishment of Nazi Germany and economic recovery based on rearmament.

The American Century covers America's rise to global superpower, Cold War dominance, and debates about hegemony, directly matching C.3.a.i on postwar US economic dominance and C.2-3 on international relations.
New Wars covers the shift to civil wars, ethnic conflicts, terrorism, and asymmetric warfare in the late 20th century, directly matching C.3.c on areas of conflict including international terrorism.

War in the 20th century covers total war, Geneva Conventions, nuclear weapons, deterrence theory, and humanitarian law, directly matching B.6 on military technology and broader themes of international warfare.

Africa's post-independence crises, Cold War interference, ethnic conflict, and the legacy of colonialism directly match C.3.c.iii on sub-Saharan Africa including superpower involvement, civil warfare, ethnic strife, and genocide.

Lenin's Bolshevik seizure of power, War Communism, and the New Economic Policy cover the ideological foundations of Soviet expansion and Cold War, matching C.1.b.i on the Nazi-Soviet pact context and broader Soviet expansionism.
